name: pdoc (Automatic Python API Documentation)

performance.notes: Documentation generation speed; depends on codebase size

agent_readiness.notes: Zero-config Python API documentation generator. pdoc mymodule. pdoc --html mymodule -o docs/. Live reload dev server: pdoc mymodule (browser auto-opens). Renders docstrings + type annotations automatically. Supports Google, NumPy, reStructuredText docstring formats. No configuration file needed. Single-file HTML output option. Much simpler than Sphinx. Markdown in docstrings. Jinja2 templates for customization. Used by mitmproxy.

gotchas: Less feature-rich than Sphinx (no Sphinx extensions ecosystem)
Cross-references to external libraries require manual configuration
Not suitable for narrative documentation (use MkDocs or Sphinx)
best_when: Auto-generating clean API reference docs from docstrings
When Sphinx is overkill for smaller projects
CI/CD documentation generation with minimal setup
avoid_when: Projects needing narrative documentation and tutorials (MkDocs better)
summary: pdoc generates clean Python API documentation from docstrings and type annotations with zero configuration. pdoc mymodule launches live-reload dev server. pdoc mymodule -o docs/ generates static HTML. Supports Google/NumPy/RST docstring formats, Markdown, Jinja2 templates. Far simpler than Sphinx — ideal for libraries needing just API reference docs without full documentation site.
